prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (the currently approved step 17 — hand from upper-left lowering the stubby pill-bottle protector tube into the DH-branded zip-seal specimen bag with QR code) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in EVERY element — same hand pose, same bag silhouette and proportions, same parted zip seal, same DIALED HEALTH bag-front logo (offset diagonal: DIALED top-LEFT + EKG-spike horizontal line + HEALTH bottom-RIGHT), same QR code, same TUBE PROPORTIONS (stubby narrow pill-bottle, height ≈ 1.5x diameter, 60/40 white-body-to-black-cap split), same overall composition, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight.

DO NOT change the tube width or height — the v4 reference's tube proportions are correct and must be preserved exactly. Do not make the tube slimmer or taller.

THE ONLY MODIFICATION — fix the embossed text on the protector tube. Currently the text on the tube appears mirrored / scrambled / hard to read. Replace it with the following clean letter-stacked arrangement so the tube reads 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly top-down:

  • BLACK CAP (top section, pinched by the hand): the word 'DIALED' is embossed running TOP-DOWN with each letter STACKED VERTICALLY one above the next, each letter in its NORMAL UPRIGHT ORIENTATION (horizontal baseline, NOT rotated, NOT sideways). The first D sits at the very TOP of the cap. Below it, the letter I. Below that, A. Below that, L. Below that, E. Below that, the final D, sitting just above the cap/body seam. So the cap shows a vertical column of six upright letters reading TOP-DOWN: D, I, A, L, E, D. Letters are OUTLINED in thin WHITE strokes against the solid black cap fill (line-art outlined letters, interior remains black — NOT solid white blocks).

  • WHITE BODY (bottom section, lower portion visible faintly through the translucent bag): the word 'HEALTH' is embossed running TOP-DOWN with each letter STACKED VERTICALLY one above the next, each letter in NORMAL UPRIGHT ORIENTATION (horizontal baseline, NOT rotated, NOT sideways). The first H sits at the TOP of the body, just below the cap/body seam. Below it, E. Below that, A. Below that, L. Below that, T. Below that, the final H, sitting near the base of the body. So the body shows a vertical column of six upright letters reading TOP-DOWN: H, E, A, L, T, H. Letters are OUTLINED in thin BLACK strokes against the white body (line-art outlined letters, interior remains white — NOT solid black blocks).

  • Combined reading top-to-bottom of the upright tube — twelve letters stacked vertically: D, I, A, L, E, D (on cap) then H, E, A, L, T, H (on body) = 'DIALED HEALTH' read linearly top-down.

  • Each letter is rendered in BOLD SANS-SERIF CAPITAL form but as an OUTLINE only (not solid-filled). Letters are sized to fit the tube's width — moderately-sized, clearly legible from the viewer's straight-on angle. Letters are NOT mirrored, NOT upside-down, NOT lying on their side — each letter is upright as normal capital letters, just arranged vertically in a single column down each section.

The hand pinches the BLACK cap above the zip seal; the lower portion of the WHITE body sits inside the bag, drawn faintly through the translucent plastic (the HEALTH letter column is muted to show through the bag but still identifiable).

Do NOT modify anything else — same hand, same bag, same zip seal, same bag-front DH logo, same QR code, same tube proportions, same composition, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic. The ONLY changes are: (1) the embossed text on the tube is now a vertical letter-stack (each letter upright, stacked one above the next, reading top-down), (2) letters are outlined line-art (not solid-filled), (3) text spells 'DIALED' on the cap and 'HEALTH' on the body with the first letter of each word at the top of its section.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers, no arrows.
